adipotide.subtitle: Prohibitin‑Targeting Peptidomimetic | Experimental Anti‑Obesity
adipotide.descriptionEn: Adipotide (Prohibitin‑TP01) is a chimeric, adipose‑vasculature–targeted peptidomimetic that homes to prohibitin/annexin A2 on white adipose tissue endothelium and delivers a pro‑apoptotic D‑(KLAKLAK)2 motif. In obese primates it produced rapid fat loss with improved insulin resistance, but development halted after an early Phase 1 oncology trial; kidney safety signals (reversible proximal tubule changes) remain a key concern.
adipotide.keyBenefits: Rapid fat‑mass reduction in obese non‑human primates with improved insulin sensitivity; direct WAT vascular targeting.
adipotide.mechanism: CKGGRAKDC homes to prohibitin/annexin A2 on white‑fat endothelium; the linked D‑(KLAKLAK)2 disrupts mitochondrial membranes after internalization, triggering localized endothelial apoptosis and adipocyte loss.

aod-9604.subtitle: Advanced Obesity Drug | Modified hGH Fragment 176-191

It stimulates lipolysis and inhibits lipogenesis without the growth-promoting effects of full hGH. Unlike intact growth hormone, AOD-9604 does not increase IGF-1, affect glucose metabolism, or bind to the GH receptor.
aod-9604.keyBenefits: Stimulates fat loss through lipolysis without growth hormone side effects. Does not increase IGF-1, affect glucose metabolism, or cause insulin resistance. Also shows promise for cartilage repair.
aod-9604.mechanism: AOD-9604 stimulates lipolysis and inhibits lipogenesis through beta-3 adrenergic receptor upregulation, without binding to the GH receptor. bioglutide.subtitle: NA-931 | Quadruple IGF-1/GLP-1/GIP/Glucagon Agonist
bioglutide.descriptionEn: Bioglutide (NA-931) is a first-in-class oral quadruple receptor agonist targeting IGF-1, GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors simultaneously. Developed by Biomed Industries, this small molecule derived from a cyclic IGF-1 fragment demonstrates remarkable oral bioavailability and blood-brain barrier penetration. Phase 2 clinical trials showed up to 13.8% body weight reduction in 13 weeks while preserving muscle mass - a significant advantage over existing GLP-1 therapies. Currently in Phase 2b/3 trials for obesity and type 2 diabetes.
bioglutide.keyBenefits: Oral administration (no injections), muscle-sparing weight loss, once-daily dosing with or without food, milder GI side effects than injectable GLP-1s, quadruple receptor activation for comprehensive metabolic effects.
bioglutide.mechanism: NA-931 is a small lipophilic molecule derived from cyclic IGF-1 fragment that crosses the blood-brain barrier. Simultaneously activates IGF-1 (muscle preservation, insulin sensitivity), GLP-1 (appetite suppression, insulin secretion), GIP (glucose metabolism, fat storage), and glucagon (energy expenditure, fat oxidation) receptors.Molecular InformationWeightProprietary (small molecule)LengthSmall

glutathione.subtitle: Master Antioxidant | Tripeptide | GSHNasal
glutathione.descriptionEn: Glutathione (GSH) is a tripeptide composed of glutamate, cysteine, and glycine, often called the body's 'master antioxidant.' Found in virtually every cell, it plays critical roles in neutralizing free radicals, detoxifying harmful substances, recycling other antioxidants (vitamins C and E), and supporting immune function. Glutathione levels decline with age and are depleted by stress, toxins, and certain diseases. Injectable forms bypass poor oral bioavailability to deliver glutathione directly to tissues.
glutathione.keyBenefits: Direct delivery bypasses poor oral bioavailability. Rapidly increases plasma glutathione levels. Supports detoxification, immune function, and antioxidant defense systems.
glutathione.mechanism: Glutathione directly neutralizes reactive oxygen species via its sulfhydryl group, conjugates toxins for excretion (Phase II detoxification), recycles vitamins C and E, and supports optimal lymphocyte function. The tripeptide's unique gamma peptide bond protects it from degradation by most peptidases.
glutathione.protocolTiming: Glutathione can be administered at any time of day. IV infusions are typically given in clinical settings. Pharmaceutical HCG is derived from the urine of pregnant women or produced recombinantly. It binds to and activates LH receptors, making it valuable for treating hypogonadism, infertility, and cryptorchidism. wolverine-stack.protocolTiming: BPC-157 has short half-life (<30 min) so twice daily dosing maintains levels. TB-500 has longer duration allowing twice weekly dosing. ghrp-6.subtitle: Growth Hormone Releasing Peptide-6 | GH Release & Cardioprotection
ghrp-6.descriptionEn: GHRP-6 is a synthetic hexapeptide and one of the first growth hormone secretagogues developed in the 1980s. It stimulates GH release by activating the ghrelin receptor (GHS-R1a) and CD36 receptor, making it one of the most extensively studied GH-releasing peptides. Unlike GHRH-based peptides, GHRP-6 works through a distinct PKC/calcium-dependent pathway. It also demonstrates notable cardioprotective, wound healing, and cytoprotective properties beyond GH release.
ghrp-6.keyBenefits: 강력한 GH 분비 촉진, 심장 보호 효과, 상처 치유 촉진, 근육 성장 지원, 식욕 증진 (그렐린 직접 활성화)
ghrp-6.mechanism: 그렐린 수용체(GHS-R1a) 및 CD36 수용체 활성화 → PKC/칼슘 의존적 경로로 뇌하수체에서 GH 방출 자극. GHRH 계열과 상이한 메커니즘으로 병용 시 시너지 효과 가능.
ghrp-6.protocolTiming: 공복 상태에서 투여 시 GH 방출 효과 극대화. 음식/인슐린 존재 시 효과 감소. CJC-1295 또는 Ipamorelin과 병용 권장.

bromantane.subtitle: Adamantane Actoprotector | Dopamine & Serotonin Synthesis Enhancer
bromantane.descriptionEn: Bromantane (Ladasten) is a unique actoprotector developed in Russia in the 1980s that combines stimulant and anxiolytic properties without causing tolerance, dependence, or withdrawal. It was approved in Russia in 1997 for the treatment of neurasthenia. Unlike classical stimulants, Bromantane works by upregulating the synthesis enzymes for dopamine and serotonin (tyrosine hydroxylase and AADC) rather than simply releasing stored monoamines. It also enhances GABAergic signaling, contributing to its anxiolytic effects.
bromantane.keyBenefits: 내성·의존성 없는 각성 효과, 항불안 + 활력 동시 개선, 도파민/세로토닌 합성 증가, 열·피로 저항성 향상, 러시아 임상 승인
bromantane.mechanism: 타이로신 수산화효소(TH) 및 방향족 아미노산 탈카르복실효소(AADC) 업레귤레이션 → 도파민·세로토닌 합성 증가. GABA성 신호전달 강화 → 항불안. 기존 자극제와 달리 모노아민을 방출하지 않으므로 소진 없음.
bromantane.protocolTiming: 오전 또는 오후 이른 시간 복용 권장 (수면 방해 가능). 음식과 함께 또는 공복 모두 가능. 저녁 늦게 복용 금지.

cardiogen.subtitle: Cardiac Bioregulatory Tetrapeptide | Cardiovascular & Tissue Repair
cardiogen.descriptionEn: Cardiogen is a bioregulatory tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Arg / AEDR) developed by Professor Vladimir Khavinson at the St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology. It belongs to the class of short peptide bioregulators ('cytomedins') designed to target cardiac tissue. Cardiogen regulates fibroblast proliferation, inhibits cardiomyocyte apoptosis via p53 downregulation, and upregulates cytoskeletal and nuclear matrix proteins. All published research is currently preclinical (in vitro and animal studies).
cardiogen.keyBenefits: 심근세포 세포사멸 억제, 섬유모세포 증식 조절, 심장 재형성 지원, 노화 관련 심혈관 기능 저하 완화 가능성
cardiogen.mechanism: p53 단백질 하향조절 → 심근세포 세포사멸 억제. 섬유모세포 증식 조절 및 세포골격/핵 기질 단백질 업레귤레이션. 심장 조직 바이오레귤레이터로 특이적 작용.
cardiogen.protocolTiming: ⚠️ 모든 프로토콜은 전임상(체외/동물) 데이터 기반. 인간 임상시험 데이터 없음. 의료진 지도하에만 사용 권장.

pnc-27.subtitle: Chimeric p53-Penetratin Anticancer Peptide | HDM-2 Targeting
pnc-27.descriptionEn: PNC-27 is a 35-amino acid chimeric peptide combining a p53 transcriptional activation domain with an Antennapedia penetratin sequence, developed by Dr. Matthew Pincus and Dr. Joseph Michl at SUNY Downstate Medical Center in 2000. It targets HDM-2 protein expressed on cancer cell membranes, forming pores that cause necrotic cell death ('poptosis') while sparing normal cells. A 2024 discovery revealed a secondary mechanism involving selective mitochondrial membrane disruption. ⚠️ The FDA has issued explicit warnings about PNC-27 use. No validated human clinical trial data exists.
pnc-27.keyBenefits: 암세포 선택적 파괴 (정상세포 보존), HDM-2 표적 독특한 메커니즘, 다양한 암종 전임상 효과 (췌장암·유방암·흑색종 등)
pnc-27.mechanism: 1차: 암세포막 표면의 HDM-2에 결합 → 막 투과 기공 형성 → 괴사성 세포사멸(poptosis). 정상세포는 막 연관 HDM-2 부재로 영향 없음. 2차 (2024): 세포 진입 후 미토콘드리아막 선택적 파괴 (리소좀 보존).
